docs.title: Introduction to ZorgAPIs
docs.text: Effective Application Programming Interfaces (APIs) achieve interoperability and are therefore of strategic importance for an Integrated Health Information System. That is why Nictiz, in consultation with the Ministry of Health, Welfare and Sport and stakeholders, has developed an API strategy for Dutch healthcare. This strategy focuses on the development of APIs towards fully standardized data exchanges.

docs.title: Resources
docs.text:
• OpenAPI specification

    API reference

  • Postman Collection

    Postman

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
Explore our documentation to gain a deeper understanding of how to harness the power of APIs in healthcare to enhance data exchange, streamline processes, and foster innovation.

  • API strategy

    This document describes the general aspects of the API strategy and the architectural frameworks in which it is embedded.

    Read docs

  • API requirements

    This specification sets out requirements for APIs in Dutch healthcare.

    Read docs

docs.title: Nictiz
docs.text: Nictiz is the Dutch competence center for electronic exchange of health and care information. Nictiz develops and maintains standards for digital information management, ensuring that healthcare information can be recorded and exchanged in an unambiguous manner. To this end Nictiz maintains close cooperation with the international Standardization Organizations: IHE, HL7, SNOMED International, PCH Alliance, CEN/NEN.

docs.title: Knowledge
docs.text:
There is a vast body of knowledge about digital information management in healthcare. As a competence center, Nictiz considers sharing this knowledge an important part of its mission.

  • Information standards

    Information standards are essential for effective, uniform information exchange in all healthcare sectors. They bridge the gap between healthcare providers, healthcare processes and IT systems.

    Read more

  • HCIMs

    The Health and Care Information Models (HCIMs) provide a structured, unambiguous way for healthcare professionals to record information and exchange it with others.

    Read more

docs.title: Code using ZorgAPIs
docs.text: Because ZorgAPIs is a community effort for the betterment of health data exchange, we welcome you to create and share tools that help users get started with ZorgAPIs API.

docs.title: Learn
docs.text: ZorgAPIs API enables the creation of applications that can interact with the API Library for Dutch Healthcare, such as retrieving API requirements, creating and managing organizations and API specifications, or publishing declarations of conformity.

docs.title: Getting started
docs.text:
• Organizations – Add organizations
  • API specifications – Add API specifications and versions

docs.title: Add API specification
docs.text:
RequestResponse 
POST https://api.sandbox.zorgapis.nl/v1alpha1/api-specifications HTTP/1.1
Content-Type: application/json

﻿{
  "isArchived": false,
  "name": "Verzamelen Huisartsgegevens", // (1)!
  "description": "Het verzamelen van huisartsgegevens: je medische dossier bij je huisarts.",
  "organizationId": "4a7c2c17-6514-46f8-aa05-6a22fb18b4ad", // (2)!
  "architecturalStyle": "REST" // (3)!
}
  1. The name of the API specification, must be unique for this organization (see "organizationId").
  2. The UUID of the organization that maintains the API specification.
  3. The architectural style of the API specification, and therefore of all associated versions. For example, "REST" or "SOAP".
HTTP/1.1 201 Created
Content-Type: application/json

﻿{
  "id": "fe30bf05-de07-4556-9b17-1f82d62fe45f", // (1)!
  "isArchived": false,
  "name": "Verzamelen Huisartsgegevens",
  "description": "Het verzamelen van huisartsgegevens: je medische dossier bij je huisarts.",
  "organizationId": "4a7c2c17-6514-46f8-aa05-6a22fb18b4ad",
  "organization": {
    "id": "4a7c2c17-6514-46f8-aa05-6a22fb18b4ad",
    "name": "Stichting MedMij"
  },
  "architecturalStyle": "REST"
}
  1. The Universally Unique Identifier, or UUID (see RFC 4122), assigned to the API specification by the API server.

docs.title: Add API specification version
Add declaration of conformity
Set API specification main version
Add organization
docs.text:
RequestResponse 
POST https://api.sandbox.zorgapis.nl/v1alpha1/organizations HTTP/1.1
Content-Type: application/json

﻿{
  "name": "Stichting MedMij", // (1)!
  "description": "MedMij is the standard in the Netherlands for the secure exchange of health data between care users and care providers.",
  "website": "https://medmij.nl/",
  "emailAddress": "info@medmij.nl",
  "phoneNumber": "+31 (0)85 303 4959",
  "address": {
    "street": "Maanweg",
    "houseNumber": 174,
    "addressLine2": "Building C/3rd floor",
    "postalCode": "2516 AB",
    "city": "The Hague",
    "stateOrRegion": "South Holland",
    "country": "NL"
  }
}
  1. The name of the organization, must be unique.
HTTP/1.1 201 Created
Content-Type: application/json

﻿{
  "id": "4a7c2c17-6514-46f8-aa05-6a22fb18b4ad", // (1)!
  "name": "Stichting MedMij",
  "description": "MedMij is the standard in the Netherlands for the secure exchange of health data between care users and care providers.",
  "website": "https://medmij.nl/",
  "emailAddress": "info@medmij.nl",
  "phoneNumber": "+31 (0)85 303 4959",
  "address": {
    "street": "Maanweg",
    "houseNumber": 174,
    "addressLine2": "Building C/3rd floor",
    "postalCode": "2516 AB",
    "city": "The Hague",
    "stateOrRegion": "South Holland",
    "country": "NL"
  }
}
  1. The Universally Unique Identifier, or UUID (see RFC 4122), assigned to the organization by the API server.

docs.title: ZorgAPIs sandbox
docs.text: Explore and experiment with our sandbox API, a secure playground for developers to test functionalities, debug code, and innovate without disrupting live data or environments.

docs.title: Resources
docs.text:
Here you will find all the tools and assets you need to get started with the ZorgAPIs API.

  • API reference – Definition of the ZorgAPIs API
  • Postman Collection – Fork our Postman Collection
